profile.Overall_Progress_Summary: Recent repeated text-based assessment evidence supports low B1 for practical written English: clear polite daily-life writing, strong B1 grammar recognition, and improving clinic/pharmacy/customer-service communication. This is not a full four-skill CEFR assessment.
profile.FullAssessment: Not completed; no voice or audio-listening sample yet.
profile.Current_Strengths: Practical written communication | Polite daily-life messages | Clinic/pharmacy/appointment language | B1 grammar recognition | Learner self-monitoring
profile.Recurring_Issues: Verb patterns | Natural phrasing | Tense choice | third-person singular -s | prepositions/function words | time/appointment precision
profile.Next_Grammar_Target: reschedule it for; will feel; ask someone to; decide whether to; consider + -ing; too + adjective + to + verb
profile.Next_Vocab_Target: reschedule, confirm, appointment, too sick to drive, forgot about, late fee, hear back, fill out
profile.Next_Pronunciation_Target: Not assessed; recommend future voice check focused on understandability.
profile.Next_ListeningSpeaking_Target: Clinic/pharmacy/customer-service roleplays with repair strategies and confirmation phrases.
profile.Preferred_Study_Mode: Short tests plus practical roleplay with mixed Chinese/English explanations
profile.Correction_Preference: Conversational correction with natural model sentences
profile.Explanation_Language_Preference: Mixed Chinese/English; Chinese explanations when helpful
profile.Voice_Mode_Preference: Not observed in recent text sessions; future voice mode recommended
profile.Next_Assessment_Due: Full four-skill assessment when learner is ready.
profile.Last_Quiz_Score: Recent partial assessments: 8/12 to 9/12 fully correct, with additional partly correct responses.
profile.Notes: Profile is conservative: keep broad CEFR as Needs Assessment until a full four-skill assessment, while surfacing repeated low-B1 practical written evidence.

recent_sessions.RecurringIssuesObserved: Tracking/package wording: 'the delivery said it already delivered' -> 'the tracking says it was delivered'. | Supportive-response grammar: 'Don’t frustrated' -> 'Don’t be frustrated'; 'you will success' -> 'you’ll succeed/pass'. | Future availability: 'I’m not be home' -> 'I won’t be home'. | Meeting phrasing: 'Do you want to meet each other?' -> 'Does that work for you?'

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: to check or investigate a problem
vocabulary.ChineseExplanation: 调查一下；帮忙查一下这个问题
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: Could you please help me look into this?
vocabulary.CommonError: May use less natural wording when describing delivery problems.

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: delivery status information for a package
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: The tracking says it was delivered, but I didn’t receive it.
vocabulary.CommonError: Learner wrote 'the delivery said it already delivered'.
vocabulary.Notes: High-value phrase for online shopping issues.

vocabulary.ExampleSentence: I think you’ll pass next time.
vocabulary.Notes: Practice succeed vs success and pass a test.

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: Is that time/plan okay for you?
vocabulary.ChineseExplanation: 这个时间/安排对你可以吗？
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: I’m free after 2 p.m. tomorrow. Does that work for you?
vocabulary.CommonError: Learner wrote 'Do you want to meet each other?'

vocabulary.ExampleSentence: I’ve already called the clinic twice today.
vocabulary.Notes: Useful for clinic and scheduling conversations.

vocabulary.ExampleSentence: I’m sorry, something came up. Could we reschedule?
vocabulary.CommonError: May need help producing a natural follow-up.

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: arrive no later than a certain time
vocabulary.ChineseExplanation: 最晚在某个时间到；某个时间之前或刚好到
vocabulary.Notes: Good example of practical nuance and learner critical thinking.

vocabulary.ExampleSentence: The clinic asked me to fill out this form before my appointment.
vocabulary.ProductionStatus: Needs more practice with verb pattern
vocabulary.Notes: Phrase is familiar; sentence pattern needs practice.

vocabulary.ExampleSentence: I emailed the clinic yesterday, but I haven’t heard back yet.

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: extra money charged because a payment is late
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: A late fee may be added if Lucy pays after June 20.
vocabulary.CommonCollocation: late fee may be added; pay a late fee
vocabulary.CommonError: Learner wrote 'payment may be added a late fee'.
vocabulary.Notes: Practice natural passive and active versions.

vocabulary.ChineseExplanation: 不小心；错误地；误收/误拿
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: Did you receive my package by mistake?
vocabulary.CommonError: Adding 'my package' makes the sentence more specific.

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: explain is the action; explanation is the thing/information given
vocabulary.ChineseExplanation: explain 是动词：解释；explanation 是名词：解释/说明
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: Could you please explain it to me? / Could you give me an explanation?
vocabulary.CommonCollocation: explain it to me; give me an explanation
vocabulary.CommonError: Learner wrote 'Could you please explanation to me.'
vocabulary.Notes: High-value distinction for service and billing conversations.

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: to change an appointment or meeting to a different time or date
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: I’d like to reschedule it for next week.
vocabulary.CommonCollocation: reschedule an appointment; reschedule it for next week
vocabulary.CommonError: Learner selected 'reschedule it' without 'for next week'.
vocabulary.Notes: Continue practicing reschedule + object + for + time.

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: to forget that something exists or is scheduled
vocabulary.ChineseExplanation: 忘了有某件事；忘了某个预约/安排
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: I forgot about my appointment, so I missed it.
vocabulary.Notes: Contrast with 'forgot to bring my insurance card'.

vocabulary.PlainEnglishMeaning: so sick that driving is not possible or not safe
vocabulary.ChineseExplanation: 病得不能开车；太不舒服不能开车
vocabulary.ExampleSentence: I’m too sick to drive today.
vocabulary.CommonCollocation: too sick to drive; too tired to cook

grammar.Description: Use this pattern to show strong certainty about a past event.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I must have left my keys at home.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 表示很确定过去发生了某事。

grammar.Description: Use 'I wish I were...' for an unreal present wish.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I wish I were more confident speaking English.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 表达现在不真实的愿望，考试/正式语法常用 were。
grammar.CommonError: I wish I am more confident.

grammar.Description: Use this passive pattern when something must be done.
grammar.ExampleSentence: The form needs to be completed before your appointment.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 表示某事需要被完成，用 needs to be + 过去分词。

grammar.Description: Use the base verb after would rather.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I’d rather stay home than go out tonight.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: would rather 后面接动词原形。
grammar.Notes: Recognition appears stable across repeated tests.

grammar.GrammarPoint: If I were you, I would + verb
grammar.Description: Use this pattern to give advice politely.
grammar.ExampleSentence: If I were you, I would call the office first.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 给建议时常用：如果我是你，我会……
grammar.CommonError: If I were you, I will call.

grammar.Description: Use 'won’t be' to talk about not being available in the future.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I won’t be home between 1 p.m. and 3 p.m.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 未来某个时间不在家，用 won’t be home。

grammar.Description: Use 'be' before adjectives after don’t in commands like 'Don’t be frustrated.'
grammar.ExampleSentence: Don’t be frustrated. Just keep practicing.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 形容词前需要 be；否定祈使句是 Don’t be + 形容词。

grammar.GrammarPoint: simple past for completed past action
grammar.Description: Use simple past for a finished action in the past.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I took the wrong bus.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 已经发生完的过去事情，用一般过去式。
grammar.CommonError: I have taken the wrong bus in a simple apology context.
grammar.Notes: Continue contrasting simple past vs present perfect.

grammar.Description: Use have/has already + past participle for actions completed before now.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I’ve already called the clinic twice today.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: already 常和现在完成时一起用，表示已经做过。

grammar.Description: After ask someone, use to + base verb.
grammar.ExampleSentence: The clinic asked me to fill out this form.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: ask someone to do something = 请/要求某人做某事。
grammar.CommonError: The clinic asked me filling out this form.

grammar.Description: Use whether/if + subject + verb in indirect questions or uncertainty statements.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I need to know whether the pharmacy is still open.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 间接问题用 whether/if + 主语 + 动词。
grammar.CommonError: I need to know whether is the pharmacy open.
grammar.Notes: Recognition appears solid in recent tests.

grammar.Description: Use 'whether to' before a verb when choosing between actions.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I’m trying to decide whether to take the bus or drive.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 决定是否做某事：decide whether to + 动词原形。

grammar.Description: Use 'arrive at' for an exact time and 'arrive by' for no later than a time.
grammar.ExampleSentence: Lucy should arrive by 10:40 a.m.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: arrive at 是具体几点到；arrive by 是最晚几点到。
grammar.Notes: Learner's reasoning showed practical understanding; arithmetic error was the main issue.

grammar.Description: Use haven’t/hasn’t + past participle + yet for something expected but not completed by now.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I haven’t heard back from the clinic yet.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 表示到现在还没有发生，用 haven’t/hasn’t + 过去分词 + yet。

grammar.Description: Use to + base verb after 'need someone'.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I need someone to help me understand this bill.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 需要某人做某事：need someone to do something。

grammar.Description: Use verb-ing after consider.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I’m considering taking an English class next month.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: consider 后面接动词时，用 -ing。

grammar.Description: Use verb-ing after interested in.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I’m interested in learning more about this class.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: interested in 后面接动词时，用 -ing。

grammar.Description: Use forgot to + base verb when you did not do something because you forgot.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I forgot to bring my insurance card.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 忘记做某事，所以没做：forgot to + 动词原形。
grammar.CommonError: I forget to bring in a past situation.
grammar.Notes: Practice matching tense to the real situation.

grammar.Description: Use either active 'may have to pay a late fee' or passive 'a late fee may be added to the payment'.
grammar.ExampleSentence: Lucy may have to pay a late fee if she pays after June 20.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 费用被加上：a fee is added to payment；或者说某人需要支付费用：someone has to pay a fee。
grammar.CommonError: Lucy’s payment may be added a late fee.

grammar.Description: Use 'be able to' plus the base verb to talk about ability or possibility.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I won’t be able to attend the appointment tomorrow.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: be able to 后面接动词原形，表示能够做某事。

grammar.Description: Use 'for' before the new time/date after reschedule.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I’d like to reschedule it for next week.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 把预约改到某个时间，用 reschedule it for + 时间。
grammar.CommonError: I’d like to reschedule it next week.

grammar.Description: Use will + base verb when predicting a future state or feeling.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I’m not sure whether I will feel better by tomorrow.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 说明明天会不会好一点，用 will feel。
grammar.CommonError: I’m not sure whether I feel better by tomorrow.
grammar.Notes: Practice with by tomorrow/by next week.

grammar.GrammarPoint: forgot about + noun vs forgot to + verb
grammar.Description: Use 'forgot about + noun' for forgetting an event exists; use 'forgot to + verb' for not doing an action.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I forgot about my appointment. / I forgot to bring my insurance card.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: forgot about + 名词 = 忘了某件事；forgot to + 动词 = 忘记去做某事。

grammar.Description: Use this pattern to mean something is too much for an action to happen.
grammar.ExampleSentence: I’m too sick to drive today.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: too + 形容词 + to + 动词，表示太……以至于不能……。

grammar.Description: Use -s after a singular third-person subject in the present tense.
grammar.ExampleSentence: Lucy needs to reply to confirm the new appointment time.
grammar.ChineseExplanation: 第三人称单数现在时要加 -s。

review_targets.reason: Learner wrote 'Could you please explanation to me.'
The clinic asked me filling out this form.
Lucy’s payment may be added a late fee.
I’d like to reschedule it next week.
I’m not sure whether I feel better by tomorrow.

review_targets.target: reschedule it for; will feel; ask someone to; decide whether to; consider + -ing; too + adjective + to + verb
reschedule, confirm, appointment, too sick to drive, forgot about, late fee, hear back, fill out
Clinic/pharmacy/customer-service roleplays with repair strategies and confirmation phrases.
